reasoning: FRED statistical analysis provides strong anchor with P(exceed) = 0.65 for Nov 2 based on linear trend and seasonal patterns. Adjusted down to 0.60 due to recent downward momentum (1 up, 3 down in last 5 points) which suggests the upward trend may be weakening. Recent Reuters data showing claims rose to 232,000 in mid-October supports upward trend thesis. Elevated continuing claims and cooling labor market (4.3% unemployment in Aug 2025) also point toward potential increases. For Nov 25 (0.55), uncertainty increases due to unclear seasonal effects late in fall. For Jan 24 (0.52), uncertainty is highest but January historically shows claim spikes, creating slight bias toward increase despite long forecast horizon.
